commit.message:
chore(changelog): record startup preop fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased startup PREOP health-poll changelog placeholder
  with the real fix commit hash

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy unreleased entries to point at the
  actual change hash before handoff
- this keeps the scenario fix commit intact while making the changelog entry
  concrete

Validation:
- git status --short

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(startup): disable startup preop health polling

Summary:
- stop passing configured health polling into slave startup when a slave is
  intentionally targeted to :preop
- add simulator scenario 47 documenting and reproducing the startup-held
  :preop regression
- register the scenario in the simulator loop and add an unreleased
  changelog entry

Rationale:
- configuration-only sessions that intentionally stop in :preop should not
  behave like active runtime watchdog sessions
- runtime-held :preop and :safeop health polling stays unchanged because the
  fix only changes the startup boundary
- Scenario: test/integration/simulator/
  47_startup_preop_health_poll_isolation_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/integration/simulator/47_startup_preop_health_poll_isolation_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/46_latched_al_error_safeop_retreat_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/05_safeop_retreat_with_health_poll_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(jumia): relax OAuth token schema to match Vendor Center API (#922)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(jumia): move logo to public root to bypass proxy interception (#921)

The proxy treats `/images/*` as storefront slug routes, so
`/images/jumia-logo.png` 404s on production. Copy the logo to
`/jumia-logo.png` (same pattern as baci-logo.svg in PR #857)
and update all remaining references.

commit.message:
Fix redis repro issues (#163)

* Fix replay shadow stack desync causing 0/10 reproduction (defect #15)

_ReplayDporScheduler.report_and_wait() was ignoring the frame argument,
so _process_opcode was never called during replay. This left the shadow
stack empty, causing _call_might_report_access to skip CALL scheduling
points for C methods on mutable objects (list.append, dict.update, etc.).
The schedule desynchronised and the critical interleaving was never
enforced during reproduction.

Fix: call _process_opcode in the replay report_and_wait before
_wait_for_turn. The _ReplayEngine is a no-op for access reporting so
this only updates the shadow stack.

Also filter _redis_replay_mode scheduling points to data commands only,
excluding connection-setup commands (AUTH, SELECT) that didn't create
scheduling points during exploration.

Affected libraries now reproduce 10/10:
- sendgrid (was 0/10), simplejwt (was 4/10), eth-ape (was 0/10),
  django-simple-history orphaned-history test (was 0/10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make Redis replay use scheduler-owned IO anchors

* Tighten IO-anchored replay semantics

* Guard IO-anchored replay scheduler reentry

* Fix ruff formatting in test files (#164)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XNhpuXbQoE4nu9RZwaK9pP

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
🔄 refactor(actions): migrate from Elastic Beanstalk to ECS deployment (#4)

* 🔄 refactor(actions): migrate from Elastic Beanstalk to ECS deployment

Removed deploy_elastic_beanstalk action and replaced with deploy-ecs action that uses
AWS ECS task definitions with automatic rollback on failure. Standardized all action
file extensions from .yml to .yaml for consistency.

Added comprehensive AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md documentation covering architecture patterns,
OIDC authentication, GitHub secrets, and development guidelines. Updated README.md with
ECS deployment examples and removed Elastic Beanstalk references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* 📝 docs(readme): add generic build and push example

Add a reusable tag-triggered workflow example for building, testing, and pushing an image to ECR.

This mirrors the consumer setup used in polymarket-discord-bot while replacing app-specific values with generic placeholders.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Without this entry, the Sales Channels nav item bypassed the
filteredNavItems permission check and was visible to all staff.
Maps it to the 'integrations' permission, same as the Integrations item.

- Replaced raw HTML with shadcn/ui (Button, Card, Badge, Input, Dialog, AlertDialog)
- Replaced confirm() with AlertDialog for disconnect confirmation
- Replaced emoji icons with Lucide icons
- Replaced inline message state with useToast()
- Added BagLoader for loading state
- Matched dashboard gradient header style from integrations page

Inline SocialMediaSchema and RegisteredAddressSchema to use local Zod v4
instead of importing from @baci/shared (which resolves to root Zod v3).
Also adds test files for sales channels components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Migrate FlatList → FlashList on products and customers screens
- Move useTheme() calls inside list item components (per RN best practices)
- Extract key extractors to module scope to prevent re-renders
- Convert falsy && patterns to ternary with null (rendering-no-falsy-and)
- Wire up server-side stock filtering in useProducts with keepPreviousData
- Remove client-side displayData filtering in products screen
- Fix active tab icon clipping with explicit dimensions and centering

- Add useMerchant hook tests

feat: add runtime qualifier support across compiler layers (#25)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Adds inert `runtime` qualifier for functions (`runtime fn`, `runtime
extern fn`) and type annotations (`runtime T`, `&runtime T`); preserved
and shown across parsing, lowering, IR, and formatted output.
* **Documentation**
* Syntax docs updated: `runtime` added as a keyword and described as an
inert modifier for functions and types.
* **Tests**
* New tests covering parsing, errors, lowering, IR/printing, and
end-to-end formatting for `runtime` markers.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

* fix: sync pnpm lockfile with current overrides config

The @tiptap/starter-kit override specifier in pnpm-lock.yaml was ^3.20.5
but package.json has ^3.20.0, causing ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_CONFIG_MISMATCH
on every CI run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deps): deduplicate @tiptap/core to fix type conflicts with puckeditor

Pin @tiptap/core override to 3.20.5 so pnpm resolves a single copy
instead of nesting a second 3.20.0 inside @puckeditor/core. This
eliminates the duplicate private-property type conflicts that required
`any` casts in the blog editor components.

- novel-editor.tsx: remove all type casts on extensions and onUpdate
- slash-command.tsx: use EditorInstance from novel instead of any,
  openProductPicker() works natively via module augmentation

commit.message:
feat(ui): redesign agent workspace surfaces

Summary:
- Reworked the prompt chain, copilot chat, and inline canvas surfaces
  into a more Obsidian-native agent workspace with clearer hierarchy,
  staged previews, and compact metadata.
- Added a visible bound-note badge in the sidebar and fixed active note
  resolution to target the actual markdown editor leaf.
- Added a minimal .gitignore for local workspace clutter and generated
  artifacts.

Rationale:
- The prior UI hid important binding and state information behind dense
  controls, which made the sidebar feel ambiguous in narrow panes.
- The new layout keeps the target, runtime, and apply flow explicit so
  preview-before-apply remains trustworthy.
- Ignoring local files prevents accidental commits of environment and
  transient workspace artifacts.

Tests:
- npm run build
- Verified installer sync across 9 vault targets

Keep target-host ranctl anchored to the declared n79 lane (#47)

The replacement request path now preserves target-host lane identity,
persists precheck artifacts, and reports blocked target-host prechecks
with declared target, profile, and rollback context instead of falling
through to nil or stub defaults.

The remote ranctl wrapper now treats blocked or invalid result statuses
as failed phases, and the replacement contract/examples now include a
schema-backed target-host lifecycle for plan/apply/verify/capture and
rollback on the declared n79 lane.

Constraint: Replacement target-host requests and status fixtures must stay aligned with the declared n79/Open5GS contract
Rejected: Keep coercing replacement backends through RanCore-only atoms | silently erased lane identity and produced stub rollback defaults

commit.message:
[raster] Guard direct SVG paint callbacks

The raster SVG image callback assumes hb_raster_paint_glyph()
seeded svg_font/svg_glyph state before the paint protocol
reaches HB_PAINT_IMAGE_FORMAT_SVG. Clients can also call
hb_font_paint_glyph_or_fail() directly with
hb_raster_paint_get_funcs(), which leaves svg_font unset and
crashes in hb_raster_svg_render().

Return false when the callback is reached without that
wrapper-only state, and cover the direct API path with a
regression test.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C build
Fixes https://github.com/harfbuzz/harfbuzz/security/advisories/GHSA-ggh9-cgg2-r4vr
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: harfbuzz
repository.description: HarfBuzz text shaping engine

seeded svg_font/svg_glyph state before the paint protocol
reaches HB_PAINT_IMAGE_FORMAT_SVG. Clients can also call
hb_font_paint_glyph_or_fail() directly with

commit.message:
[gpos] Guard cursive attachment reversal

Cursive attachment reversal clears the old child edge before
walking the existing chain and reinstalling reversed links.
If the chain distance does not stay in range for
attach_chain(), reinstalling the reversed edge can truncate
and leave a poisoned chain value.

Pass the buffer length into the reversal helper so it drops
out-of-bounds chains, and use a round-trip fit check before
storing the reversed distance. When the reversed distance does
not fit, leave the old edge detached instead of corrupting
the chain.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C build --print-errorlogs
Fixes https://issues.chromium.org/issues/496302307
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: harfbuzz
repository.description: HarfBuzz text shaping engine

[gpos] Guard cursive attachment reversal

Cursive attachment reversal clears the old child edge before
walking the existing chain and reinstalling reversed links.
If the chain distance does not stay in range for
attach_chain(), reinstalling the reversed edge can truncate

commit.message:
feat: Indexing Status Dialog with detailed error diagnostics (#96)

* docs: add indexing status dialog implementation plan

* test: scaffold indexing status dialog helpers

* feat: add indexing status dialog shell

* fix: tighten indexing status dialog source row rendering

* feat: lazily create indexing status dialog

* feat: add indexing status entry points

* feat: collect detailed indexing errors

* fix: align indexing error details with source status

* feat: forward indexing error details to dialog

* test: align indexing error details app test name

* feat: show recent indexing errors

* fix: truncate long error messages and add overflow accessibility

* fix: increase indexing status dialog height to 640px

* fix: truncate indexing error messages on utf-8 boundaries

* fix: reduce source count pill badge vertical size

* fix: rename "Skipped" to "Already Indexed" in indexing status dialog

"Skipped" had negative connotations (rejected/ignored). "Already Indexed"
is neutral and accurate: these sessions are in the index and were not
re-scanned because their fingerprint was unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale TODO and #[allow(dead_code)] on errors_detail field

The field is actively used by the indexing status dialog (Phase 2),
so the suppression and TODO(task-6) comment are no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "docs: add indexing status dialog implementation plan"

This reverts commit 6bd3a98a816fa8045504afed8ecea27d4ce06c07.

* fix: animate progress bar pulse during reindexing

pulse() was called only once in sync_widgets, so the bar never visibly
moved. Now a 250ms timer drives the animation while indexing is active
and is cleaned up when indexing completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add doc comments to structs and enums in indexing_status.rs

Document non-obvious fields and types: lazy dialog creation, view-state
pattern, pulse timer, badge/subtitle rules, and error section constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reverse indexing error list to show most recent errors first

cf https://github.com/supermaciz/sessions-chronicle/pull/96#discussion_r2991986075

* fix: keep newest indexing errors with rolling cap and show most recent first

Replace early-return in push_indexing_error with remove(0) so the error
buffer discards the oldest entry instead of silently dropping new ones.
Reverse the iterator in build_recent_error_rows to display newest errors
first in the Recent Errors section.

Addresses PR #96 review comments r2991986070 and r2991986075.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clarify indexing status recent errors

Rewrite generic Recent Errors messages to include the AI assistant and add coverage for the new formatting.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: skip empty claude sessions

Treat Claude Code sessions with no messages as ignorable during indexing while still surfacing genuinely malformed session files as errors.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: format generated config template

* fix(indexer): use VecDeque for O(1) error buffer operations

Replace Vec with VecDeque for the error_detail buffer to avoid O(n)
remove(0) operations when capping errors at MAX_INDEXING_ERRORS.
Now uses pop_front() for efficient FIFO eviction.

fix(ui): correct pluralization in overflow message

Show 'and 1 more error' instead of 'and 1 more errors' when exactly
one error remains beyond the visible limit.

* chore(ide): add Meson build directory profile in misc.xml

* fix: address PR #96 review findings

Move test-only _incremental indexer wrappers to #[cfg(test)] impl block,
remove spurious #[allow(dead_code)] on errors_detail field, document
qualify_error_message coupling, and treat NoUserMessages as empty session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace unavailable emblem-ok-symbolic with object-select-symbolic

emblem-ok-symbolic does not exist in the Adwaita icon theme; it only
appears in HighContrast without a -symbolic variant. Use the correct
Adwaita checkmark icon instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): consume published lean-exposition binary

Stop checking out and rebuilding lean-exposition inside the Exposition workflow. Instead, pin a published lean-exposition commit, download the matching Linux x86_64 artifact from its Publish Exposition Binary workflow, verify the packaged lean-toolchain, and run the extracted exposition binary directly under lake env.

Validation: lake exe cache get and lake build BridgelandStability succeeded in a clean worktree. lake exe runLinter was blocked locally by an ld.lld bus error while linking runLinter:exe on this aarch64 machine. The upstream lean-exposition publish workflow for e8fa65d41c5e8d3dc14f183dd30b97f784116bf2 succeeded, and the artifact download/extract plus lean-toolchain verification path was exercised locally. Executing the published binary locally is not possible on this machine because the artifact is Linux x86_64 and the host is aarch64.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(ci): publish prebuilt exposition binaries

Add a packaging script and a dedicated workflow that builds the Linux exposition binary, uploads it as a commit-addressed artifact on pushes to master, and attaches the same assets to tagged releases. Also validate packaging in the existing CI workflow and document the downstream download contract in the README.

Validated with lake build exposition and a local packaging run via ./scripts/package-exposition-binary.sh.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): remove invalid exposition cache step

Drop the lake exe cache get invocation from the lean-exposition checkout in the Exposition workflow. That repo does not provide a cache executable, so the job was failing before it ever built the exposition tool.

Validated with lake exe cache get, lake build BridgelandStability, lake exe runLinter, lake build exposition in /home/matt.linux/lean/lean-exposition, and a fresh exposition generation run against BridgelandStability.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: share snapshot script orchestration

commit.message:
fix: harden macOS focus, hook sync, and local server reliability

- throttle macOS focus and simplify AppleScript execution
- harden hook installation with atomic writes and version-aware cleanup
- add dynamic local port discovery plus signed health checks
- gracefully handle oversized permission payloads
- add installer compatibility tests and doc updates

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Gemini <noreply@google.com>

repository.name: clawd-on-desk
repository.description: A desktop pet that reacts to your Claude Code sessions in real-time — thinking, typing,  juggling, sleeping, and more.

commit.message:
docs: clarify required AI provenance disclosure

Spell out that all AI-assisted commits must disclose AI use, require exact co-author trailers for Codex and Claude when they materially author or edit patches, and require every applicable trailer when multiple AI systems contribute. Also record non-authoring AI help truthfully in the commit body or footer.

Validation: lake exe cache get succeeded. lake build BridgelandStability is currently blocked by an existing parse error at BridgelandStability/Deformation/DeformedGtLe.lean:444 (unexpected token 'GL'), so lake exe runLinter was not run.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(tasks): backfill board context on legacy card moves

Ensure update_task restores board context before lane automation so legacy tasks without board_id still resolve the default board and trigger entry automation correctly.

fix(rust): merge specialist locale overlays

Add locale-aware overlay merging to the Rust specialist loader and desktop /api/specialists responses, with tests covering bundled overlay fallback semantics.

Refs #233

commit.message:
fix(rust): honor specialist locale overlays in desktop api

Align Rust specialist loading with locale overlay semantics by merging base definitions with locale-specific YAML overlays and wiring /api/specialists to accept locale-aware responses.

Refs #233

commit.message:
Remove blueprint integration and add exposition CI

Drop the LeanArchitect dependency, remove the BridgelandBlueprint wrapper library and blueprint workflow, trim the project homepage links, and update extractDeps plus the new exposition workflow to use the slimmer library surface. Validated with lake exe cache get, lake build BridgelandStability, lake exe runLinter, and a fresh exposition run against the cleaned target.

AI-assisted via Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[claude] feat: enforce preflight validation before pipeline (#194)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(ui): redesign chess game interface

Summary:
- rebuild the main game view around a board-first layout with a
  compact top bar, integrated player strips, and a quieter side rail
- replace the shared visual system with a lighter refined-modern
  palette, updated typography, and cleaner responsive behavior
- refresh the setup, waiting, clock, and move history surfaces so the
  full play flow reads as one cohesive UI

Rationale:
- the previous UI gave the board, side rail, and status chrome nearly
  equal visual weight, so the game itself never felt primary
- regrouping controls and reducing surface noise makes casual play
  easier to scan while preserving existing functionality

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: improve hook runtime test failure summaries

Prefer real Vitest failure headers in fitness summaries and silence noisy hook-runtime test logs.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5) <codex@openai.com>
